Title: App search and Ctrl K
Summary: Press Ctrl K from anywhere to open the global search. What it indexes and what it deliberately does not.
Body:
## The shortcut

Press :kbd[Ctrl+K] (or :kbd[Cmd+K] on macOS) from anywhere in the app to focus the search input. It lives in the left **sidebar**, below the four meta-action icons (Diagnostics / Preferences / Help / Restart) and above the domain nav links. Whatever you typed last time is still there, selected.

The results dropdown grows rightward over the main content area, anchored to the input's left edge.

## How matching works

The search uses substring matching, not fuzzy matching. Typing `edd` finds `Eddie`. Typing `eedi` does not, because the characters are not contiguous. This is a deliberate choice for a single author corpus where you know your own ids: predictability beats typo tolerance, and fuzzy matching surfaced too many false positives at the threshold that caught real typos.

Matches are ranked by position (matches near the start of a string score better) and by haystack length (shorter strings score better when both match). So `edd` ranks Eddie above an Eddie themed karma impact, even though both contain the substring.

## What is indexed

- NPC display names and ids
- Item display names and slugs
- Quest titles and ids
- Karma impact ids
- Faction display names and enum names
- Dialog sequence ids (folder shows as side context)
- Balloon text (this is the principled exception: balloons have no Id field in C#, so Text is the identity)
- Codex entry display names and ids
- Shader basenames (parent folder shows as side context)
- Static pages (Game concept, Assets, Diagnostics, Preferences, and Help)

## What is not indexed

- Description prose, dialog line text, quest body, codex body, help body. The reason is signal to noise: a search for "scavenger" should find the Scavengers faction at the top, not bury it under a dozen lines of dialog that mention the word.

## Help is searched separately

Help entries are not in the global palette. The [Help page](/help) has its own search at the top that does index Body text, because once you are inside Help, full text coverage is the right tradeoff.

## Keyboard

- :kbd[Ctrl+K] focus the input
- :kbd[ArrowDown] / :kbd[ArrowUp] walk results
- :kbd[Enter] pick the active row (or the first row if you have not moved yet)
- :kbd[Escape] clear the query, or close the dropdown if already empty
